primary_concern: Sepsis risk with multi-organ dysfunction and acute kidney injury (AKI) progression
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (WBC 14.2 K/uL) and hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL meet Sepsis-3 criteria. Platelets have declined to 88 K/uL (from 95 K/uL) with persistent thrombocytopenia. AKI progressed from Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) within 24 hours. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggests possible hepatic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine rose 6.5 mg/dL in 6 hours (from 2.4 to 8.9 mg/dL), exceeding KDIGO Stage 3 criteria
Platelets <100 K/uL meet SOFA coagulation score ≥3 (platelets <50 K/uL threshold)

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis bundle: broad-spectrum antibiotics, fluid resuscitation, and source control investigation
Assess for vasopressor requirements to evaluate septic shock criteria
Order urgent nephrology consultation for AKI Stage 3 management
Consider platelet transfusion if thrombocytopenia <50 K/uL persists
Monitor bilirubin trends for possible hepatic dysfunction
doctor_handoff: Review for sepsis source (urine/blood cultures pending), reassess MAP and lactate trends for shock criteria, and evaluate for reversible AKI causes (e.g., hypovolemia, nephrotoxins).

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL, bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
Lactate elevated to 4.2 mmol/L; AKI Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL); platelets 95 K/uL
AKI progressed to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL); platelets 88 K/uL; persistent thrombocytopenia

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
